channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Supporting First-in-Family Students to Successfully Graduate from University
item.description: In Australia, roughly 30% of new undergraduates are the first in their families to attend university, meaning their parents do not hold tertiary qualifications. These students often face unfamiliar ch...

item.title: Police seize P9.5 million worth of shabu in Lucena and Zamboanga anti-drug operations
item.description: Philippine authorities successfully seized a total of P9.5 million worth of shabu from operations in Lucena City and Zamboanga City over the weekend. In Lucena, police conducted a sting in Barangay Ib...

item.title: Nigeria Crude Oil Exports to US Drop Sharply in January 2026 Amid Global Energy Challenges
item.description: For January 2026, Nigeria see big drop for crude oil wey e dey export go United States. U.S. Census Bureau and Bureau of Economic Analysis show say imports from Nigeria fall almost 47 per cent from De...

item.title: Partner of Scottish gang leader Steven Lyons detained in Dubai amid international arrests
item.description: Amanda Lyons, the partner of notorious Scottish gang leader Steven Lyons, was arrested in Dubai in connection with alleged offences committed in Spain. Her detention followed an Interpol red alert iss...

item.title: Guidelines for Applying to Startup Battlefield 2026
item.description: TechCrunch's Startup Battlefield 2026 seeks the most promising early-stage startups rather than the most polished. The program highlights companies with unique, category-defining ideas that can meanin...

item.title: President Samia acknowledges gains in public financial management and calls for further reforms
item.description: President Samia Suluhu Hassan has expressed satisfaction with improvements in Tanzania’s public financial management, following the latest report from the Controller and Auditor General (CAG). The rep...

item.title: Family mourns Chloe Watson Dransfield following fatal stabbing in Leeds
item.description: The family of 16-year-old Chloe Watson Dransfield, who tragically died after being stabbed in Leeds, have publicly paid tribute to her. Chloe, from Gomersal, West Yorkshire, was found unconscious on K...

item.title: South Africa’s NHI Faces Legal Delays as Government Seeks Dialogue with Doctors and Business
item.description: Health Minister Aaron Motsoaledi has opened discussions with the South African Medical Association (SAMA), one of several organisations challenging the National Health Insurance (NHI) Act in court. Th...

item.title: Israel Approves Death Penalty Law for Palestinian Terror Suspects
item.description: Israel's parliament, on Monday, gave the green light to a controversial bill allowing the execution of Palestinians convicted of terror-related offenses resulting in fatalities. The move, pushed by Na...

item.title: NATO Intercepts Fourth Iranian Missile Entering Turkish Airspace
item.description: NATO don intercept one ballistic missile wey Iran launch wey enter Turkish airspace on Monday. Dis na di fourth missile wey NATO don stop since di wahala start. Turkish Defence Ministry confirm say di...

item.title: Goa's Wetlands Face Mounting Pressure from Development and Tourism
item.description: Goa's delicate wetland ecosystems are facing increasing threats from rapid development and expanding tourism, raising concerns about their long-term sustainability. Despite a Supreme Court mandate req...

item.title: Kasipul MP Aspirant Advocates for Monthly SHA Payments to Support Vulnerable Kenyans
item.description: Homa Bay’s Kasipul parliamentary aspirant, Newton Ogada, has raised concerns over the Social Health Authority (SHA) payments, urging the government to make them affordable and equitable. Speaking to T...

item.title: Midgitte Bardot: Challenging Prejudice and Empowering Through Drag
item.description: Tamm Reynolds, a non-binary trans performer with dwarfism, has gained recognition for their alter ego, Midgitte Bardot, through daring and provocative performances. Their latest show, 'Shooting From B...

item.title: President Ruto reassures Kenyans of stable fuel and fertiliser supply despite Middle East conflict
item.description: President William Ruto has reassured Kenyans that the country has enough fertiliser and fuel supplies despite disruptions caused by the ongoing conflict in the Middle East. Speaking after a high-level...

item.title: Education Initiatives Transform Johannesburg's Western CBD into a Tech and Learning Hub
item.description: Johannesburg's western CBD, once the bustling heart of the city's financial and mining sectors, has been facing decades of urban decay as businesses relocated north. Many of the historic office buildi...

item.title: Mental Fatigue Linked to Intensive AI Use in Work Environments
item.description: As artificial intelligence tools become more integrated into workplaces, some users are experiencing a phenomenon described by consultants at Boston Consulting Group as 'AI brain fry.' This state of m...

item.title: PCCB reports irregularities in nearly half of public projects monitored
item.description: The Prevention and Combating of Corruption Bureau (PCCB) has disclosed that out of 1,864 public projects monitored, 913 showed some form of irregularities. This was revealed by Director General Crispi...

item.title: PNP Confiscates Over P2 Billion in Smuggled Cigarettes Nationwide in Early 2026
item.description: From January 1 to March 29, 2026, the Philippine National Police (PNP) conducted a total of 3,153 anti-smuggling operations across the country, resulting in the confiscation of smuggled cigarettes val...

item.title: U.S. Army Reviews Video of Apache Helicopter Near Kid Rock’s Residence
item.description: The U.S. Army has launched an investigation into a video showing an Apache attack helicopter flying low near singer Kid Rock’s Nashville home. The video, posted by Kid Rock on social media, shows the ...

item.title: PNP enforces discipline as 740 police face sanctions in first quarter of 2026
item.description: The Philippine National Police (PNP) announced that 740 of its personnel were subjected to administrative sanctions between January 1 and March 25, 2026, as part of its effort to promote accountabilit...
